title: phlegmariurus hillianus
intro: Phlegmariurus hillianus, also known as the climbing forked polypody, is a species of climbing fern native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Phlegmariurus hillianus can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
